title: Epic: Canonical derived-asset readiness and archive-risk control
description:
Background: The report found that agents are forced to reconcile DB existence, scan watermarks, lexical metadata, semantic readiness, quarantines, and source-path availability by hand. The same host can be stale-but-searchable, missing lexical metadata, high archive risk, semantic absent, or partially quarantined, and current surfaces do not consistently say which state dominates.

Goal: provide one canonical readiness model shared by health, status, triage, search metadata, doctor, and swarm/fleet surfaces. It must distinguish usable stale indexes from true outages, and it must make high archive-risk states impossible to miss before repair commands.

Acceptance: robot JSON exposes a derived-asset truth table and safe next command; stale-but-searchable states are not treated as missing data; archive-risk hosts require backup/inspection guidance; tests cover local stale, ts1 archive-risk, csd missing metadata, ts2 timeout-prone status, semantic absent, and quarantine-excluded conversations.

dependencies.title: Epic: Operational resilience from 2026-06-08 CASS fleet/session analysis

dependents.title: Add safe/unsafe next-command envelope to readiness outputs
Make archive-risk states backup-first and hard to misuse
Wire shared readiness into health status triage and search metadata
Build readiness fixture matrix from the 2026-06-08 fleet states

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Implementation starting points: src/search/readiness.rs already defines a partial readiness vocabulary but comments say it is not fully wired. src/lib.rs builds health/status/triage/search robot payloads. src/doctor.rs and doctor_chokepoint/doctor_runs carry archive-risk concepts. Treat this epic as the unification layer.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Closure-proof overlay from the full bead review: do not close this bead on implementation claims or prose-only evidence. If it changes code or user-visible behavior, closure must cite the relevant row(s) from the .12.1 test matrix, include unit/integration coverage where applicable, include bounded E2E or golden proof for robot/human surfaces, and produce structured logs/artifact manifests per .12.3/.12.6. If this bead is design/docs-only, closure must explicitly say why runtime tests are not applicable and still cite reviewed docs/goldens or proof artifacts. Preserve all requested functionality; do not narrow scope to make tests easier.
